name: Glider
flavorText: Through feather, scale, or skein, you echo your ancestor’s ability to soar
description: So long as you are not restrained,  incapacitated, or equipped with Bulky items or armor, you may choose to glide when falling, moving up to 40ft horizontally for each 10ft you descend. While gliding, you take no damage from falling.

name: Leap
flavorText: Your ancestors have gifted you with long, powerful legs capable of leaping great distances
description: You can jump up to 10 feet into the air. With a running start, you can jump to a Near location. You ignore the first 20ft of falling damage.

name: Natural Weapons
flavorText: With spine, tooth, claw or horn, you inherit your ancestor’s capacity for violence
description: While unarmed, your attacks are treated as 2d6 and not Impaired.

name: Natural Armor
flavorText: Shell, scale, or hide protected your ancestors from danger, as they protect you now
description: Gain +1 Armor (Max 4)

name: Natural Climber
flavorText: Gifted with your ancestors surefootedness, you ascend
description: You can climb sheer surfaces that others would find impossible without special equipment

name: Diminutive
flavorText: Your ancestors were small and quick, traits advantageous for a Delver
description: You are small in size, under 4ft tall, allowing you to squeeze into places other cannot reach and hide where others could not conceal themselves. Gain a Boon to Action rolls where your small size would be advantageous.

name: Powerful
flavorText: Your ancestors were great in stature and strength
description: You tower over most other folk. You may treat one item you carry as if it did not have the Bulky trait.

name: Aquatic
flavorText: Your ancestors came from the water, and the depths hold no danger to you
description: You gain Swimming as a skill and may freely move and act underwater. You can stay underwater for up to an hour without needing to breathe.

name: Burrower
flavorText: The earth was your ancestors’ home Where others feel fear, you feel comfort
description: You can dig freely through dirt and clay without tools, and can tunnel through earth at a quarter of your normal speed. Gain the Underground domain.

name: Swift
flavorText: Your ancestors were fleet of hoof and paw, their speed lives on in you
description: Spend 1 Stress to catch up to or outpace another creature for a few moments.

name: Favored Weapon
flavorText:
The Warrior’s weapon is an extension of their body.
-The Path of the Warrior, meditation II, verse 1
description: Choose Ranged or Melee. While wielding a weapon of that type, you increase the weapon die by 1 step. (e.g. d6 > d8). Gain the Warfare domain.

name: Battlecraft
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time: Warfare, Athletics, Crafting, Monsters, Tactics

name: Focused Training
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time: Climbing, Swimming, Acrobatics, Smithing, Intimidation

name: Terrifying Display
description: You gain Intimidation as a skill. Once per session, when you defeat or critically injure an opponent with a melee attack, you may choose to make all other opponents immediately make a Morale Roll with a Bane.

name: Trick Shot
description: Gain Trick Shooting as a skill. Once per session, when you make a Gambit with a Ranged attack, you can treat the die as if it were an 8 and a Melee attack.

name: Gatecrasher
description: Gain Demolition as a skill. Once per session, through ingenuity or sheer might, you may destroy a physical obstacle or object of up to twice your size that is preventing you from achieving your goal. The destruction is not quiet or subtle.

name: Smite
flavorText:
Might or magic, make each strike a killing blow.
-The Path of the Warrior, meditation III, verse 2
description: Spend 2 Stress to add +d12 or Blast to an attack made with a weapon

name: Fueled by the Fallen
flavorText:
To deny death at the expense of another is to truly feel alive.
-Warlord Nale of the Lapine Empire
description: Whenever you defeat or critically injure an opponent while fighting for your life, clear 1 Stress.

name: Vicious Momentum
flavorText:
One defender fell after another, a cascade leading to an inevitable conclusion.
-Scholar Sirius Olan, survivor of the Sack of Wyrmwood Crossing
description: Whenever you defeat an opponent, roll the weapon die again and inflict that damage on another opponent Close to the original opponent.

name: Brutal Attacks
description: You may re-roll one die in your Attack Pool before choosing the highest die to use for damage.

name: Fighting Style
description: Choose a fighting style when you gain this talent. You may gain other fighting styles as Minor Upgrades.

name: Last Stand
flavorText:
…Who's next..?
-Final Words of Redwynne Letteer, Veteran Delver
description: Until the end of the current encounter, your Body, Mind and Spirit attributes become 18, cannot be lowered by any means, and you cannot die until the greatest threat to you and your party has been defeated or driven off. At the conclusion of the encounter, you may have one final scene before your character succumbs to their wounds.

name: Avatar of War
flavorText:
The scale of destruction… That was no kin. That was a force of nature.
-Scholar Sirius Olan, survivor of the Sack of Wyrmwood Crossing
description: Fueled by rage and fell magicks, you become a force of nature that destroys everything in your path. You lay waste to an entire landmark, dungeon, or point of interest before dying in a cacophony of violence. There are no survivors.

name: Tough
flavorText:
The Underground is not for the soft of mind or body.
-Aspirant’s Guide to Delving, Chapter 2
description: Your maximum Guard increases by 4. Gain the Wilds or Underground domain.

name: Wilderlore
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time: Wilds, Animals, Healing, Monsters, Underground, Survival

name: Fieldcraft
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time: Hunting, Herbalism, Navigation, Cooking, Climbing, Scouting, Surgery, Swimming

name: Protect by Example
flavorText:
Pay attention and only step where I step…
-Lyn Spriggan, Veteran Delver
description: When you succeed on a Save to avoid danger, all nearby allies gain a Boon on their next Action roll to avoid the same danger.

name: Selfless Protector
flavorText:
Bear the pain of your fellow Delver and bring them safely home.
-Aspirant’s Guide to Delving, Chapter 4
description: Once per scene, you may protect a Nearby ally when they take damage. You take that damage instead.

name: Tunnel Fighter
flavorText:
Danger comes from all directions at all times.
-Aspirant’s Guide to Delving, Chapter 2
description: You cannot be surprised or taken off-guard by other creatures or traps. Damage from sources that you are unaware of always deals damage to your Guard before affecting your Attributes.

name: Vigilance
flavorText:
Danger comes from all directions at all times.
-Aspirant’s Guide to Delving, Chapter 2
description: You cannot be surprised or taken off-guard by other creatures or traps. Damage from sources that you are unaware of always deals damage to your Guard before affecting your Attributes.

name: Field Medic
flavorText:
Chew this, it won’t stop the poison, but it will slow it down.
-Lyn Spriggan, Veteran Delver
description: Gain Medicine as a skill. Once per session, restore d4 to an ally’s Body or Speed as you perform first aid even with limited supplies or scavenged substances.

name: Negate
flavorText:
A defense for every offense, a counter for every strike.
-Inscription on the shield of Warden Philippa
description: Spend 2 Stress to remove one die of your choice from an enemy’s attack pool after all dice have been rolled.

name: Trapper
description:
You have learned how to create traps that can be triggered by pressure plates or tripwires. Spend 2 Stress to place one of the following traps while you have a few minutes to spare:

Spike Trap
The triggering creature is struck by spring-loaded spikes for 2d8 damage.

Snare Trap
The triggering creature is Restrained, and unable to until the trap is destroyed.

Alarm Trap
A loud noise is emitted when a creature triggers the trap.

name: Final Haven
flavorText:
What is the price of one soul for the survival of an entire community?
-Warden Krigg, moments before his martyrdom at Wyrmwood Crossing
description: Sacrificing yourself, you prevent one haven or landmark from succumbing to encroaching destruction. The area becomes a new haven, protected for years to come from anything that would threaten it or its community.

name: Well Read
flavorText:
Knowledge is a weapon. Arm yourself well.
-Aspirant’s Guide to Delving, Chapter 2
description: Gain two of the following knowledge related domains: History, Technology, Monsters, Magic, Religion. Additionally, by spending an hour of time pouring over your notes and books, you can gain one Domain of your choice until you use this ability again. You cannot use this ability again until you rest for a night in a safe location.

name: Flash of Insight
flavorText:
In that moment of perfect clarity, I knew what needed to be done. Gods forgive me.
-Memoirs of the Alabaster Sage, vol VI
description: You can spend 1 Stress to make an Action Roll as if you had training in a relevant skill.

name: Studious
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time: History, Technology, Animals, Monsters, Magic, Religion, Occult, Government, Underground

name: Applied Education
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time: Alchemy, Astronomy, Astrology, Enchanting, Cartography, Cooking

name: Chef
description: Gain the Cooking skill. Once per session, when you have about an hour to spare, you can prepare a meal for your allies with whatever you have at hand. Each player character gains a Snack. Anyone who eats the Snack clears 2 Stress without needing to make an Action roll.

name: Analyze
description: Once per Situation, you can analyze your surroundings or a creature to discover a clue or an important bit of information that will be helpful in the near future. E.g. A monster’s weakness, signs of a trap, the probable purpose of an artifact, etc.

name: Delver's Cant
description: Gain the Linguistics skill. You have created a secret language and shared it with your allies, allowing your party to communicate simple ideas, plans, and directions aloud without revealing your intentions.

name: Backup Plan
description: Once per session, when another character fails and action roll or save, you allow them to re-roll with 1 Boon. Tell the GM how you planned, prepared, or accounted for this situation.

name: Hyperfocused
description: You are adept in shutting out distractions while focusing on a task. When performing an Action Roll in pitched battle or another dangerous situation, your hands are steady and your mind is focused, you ignore any Banes that would apply to the roll.

name: Ritualist
description:
You have learned how to evoke magical effects through esoteric or forgotten rituals. Rituals do not require magical talent or divine connection. Instead, they require the right knowledge, symbols, items, and time. Spend 2 stress to perform one of the rituals you know.
Choose one of the rituals from the following Minor advances to learn when you first pick this Major talent:

name: Mind Palace
description:
You have learned a technique that allows you to retreat into a mentally constructed room within your mind, where you can analyze problems and deduce solutions with eerie precision. Spend 2 Stress to enter your Mind Palace for several minutes. While in your palace:
You are blind and deaf to your surroundings
You can perfectly visualize anything or anyone you have met,  experienced, read, or learned and organize your thoughts to draw conclusions. 
You may ask the GM if any of these items are connected, and the GM will answer truthfully.

name: Tenure
description: You retire your character and they become an NPC. The Guild recognizes you as too important of an asset to risk on Delves into the field and offers you a career as a Professor. Choose one of your Domains or Skills. You teach classes focused on that Domain or Skill to prepare the next generation of Delvers. The remaining party can call upon you once for a grand favor within the capacity of you or the Guild to grant.

name: Ultimate Revelation
description: Your studies have revealed one of the ultimate truths that lies behind the facade of reality. You learn the true Name of an entity, object, or concept and can invoke that Name to assert complete control over it for monumental but fleeting moment. Invoking the name comes at a great cost, as mortal minds were never meant to comprehend such things, and your character’s mind is forever shattered, rendering you catatonic for the rest of your life.

name: Minor Arcana
flavorText:
The Aether gives us unlimited potential. Shape it to your will.
-Lectures of Professor Yanwu, Veteran Guildmage
description:
Gain the Magic domain. You can cast a minor spell for one of the following effects for as long as you concentrate. Distractions, such as taking Guard damage, will break your concentration.
Move or manipulate a nearby object as if you were using your own hand
Conjure an arcane light to illuminate the nearby area
Create a noise or illusory image in a nearby location
Detect and analyze nearby sources of magic or supernatural power. The longer you concentrate, the more information the GM will provide.
Light or extinguish a nearby flame or magitech light source, no concentration required.

name: Esoterica
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time.

name: Magecraft
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time.

name: Arcane Deflection
flavorText:
The sharp mind can turn aside steel.
-Bastian proverb
description: Once per scene, Impair a ranged or melee attack made against you.

name: Essence Extraction
flavorText:
Loathe though I was to destroy a sacred object, it was a necessary sacrifice. I’m sure the people whose lives I saved in return share my sentiment.
-Testimony of Atticus Weaver, Veteran Guildmage
description: Gain the Technology domain. You may sacrifice a Curio you are carrying to ignore the Stress cost of casting a spell, up to 3 Stress.

name: Aethersense
flavorText:
Sort of tickles the nose, like ozone, but a smell that you can see…
-Miriam Lidl, Survey Clade Aspirant
description: You can sense any magic effects or Aetherstone nearby without concentrating. Different types of magic feel different to you.

name: Arcane Blast
flavorText:
It is simpler to use Aether to destroy than create. Both are necessary.
-Lectures of Professor Yanwu, Veteran Guildmage
description: Spend 1 Stress to cast this spell. Pick a damage type (fire, lightning, toxic, etc) and make a ranged attack (d10, Far) using that damage type. Spend 2 additional Stress to give the attack the Blast tag.

name: Arcane Telekinesis
flavorText:
The spatial manipulation of objects is a fundamental application of the Arcane arts, yet many fail to develop it to its full potential. In this essay–
-Treatise of Ysgrin Farflinger, Veteran Guildmage
description:
Spend 1 Stress to cast this spell. Choose a nearby object up to half your size and weight. While you concentrate on this spell, you can lift, manipulate, move, or hurl the object (d6 through d10 based on size) anywhere nearby. You may choose a new object to focus on at any time while concentrating, but doing so causes the previous object to fall inert to the ground.
Spend 1 additional Stress to target an object up to twice your size.
Spend 1 additional Stress to target a creature instead of an object, within the same size limits.

name: Arcane Familiar
flavorText:
I grew tired of hiring idle peons for menial labor, so I simply conjured my own. The lack of complaints is quite refreshing.
-Malaphorad Gladfrost, Mage-Entrepreneur
description:
You gain a Familiar, a tiny spirit that follows your spoken or mental commands. 
Familiar: 6 Body, 12 Speed, Mind/Spirit equal to yours, 2 Guard
You define the appearance and demeanor of your familiar, and while your familiar is within Far range of you, you may close your eyes and ears to the world to view and sense the world from its perspective. 
You may dismiss and resummon your familiar at will with a quick incantation, provided you have an Arcane Focus.
If your familiar is slain, it discorporates into Aether and can be resummoned by spending 1 Stress during a ten-minute ritual.

name: Supernova
description: You have learned the means to cast the ultimate destructive spell. When you cast this spell, define a distance between Near and 1 mile, centered on yourself. Everything within that radius, including yourself, is annihilated.

name: Divine Guidance
flavorText:
The divine do not speak to you, but through you.
-Teachings of Saint Marzen
description:
Pick a deity to follow. Gain the Rites of Your God as a Skill. Gain the Religion domain. Gain the following abilities:
Bless: Spend 1 Stress to grant yourself or a nearby Ally 1 Boon on an Action Roll or Save.
If you spend a Blessing to use this ability, the recipient restores d4 Spirit
Minor Upgrade: If you spend a Blessing to use this ability, the recipient restores d4 of any Attribute.
Augury: Spend 2 Stress to perform an Augury ritual and beseech your God for direction and describe a planned course of action. Your God will indicate if the course of action will bring Hope (positive outcome), Fear (negative outcome), Both, or Neither. Once you perform an Augury, your God will no longer answer further Auguries until you present a suitable offering.

name: Seminary
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time.

name: Rites of Knowledge
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time.

name: Communion
flavorText:
Bread nourishes the body. Service nourishes the soul.
-Fifth Tenet of The Final Judge
description: Gain the Religion domain. Restore d6 Spirit whenever you visit a temple or shrine to your God and perform a sacred rite or preach to an audience.

name: Rebuke the Unholy
flavorText:
To the false life, I rebuke thee! To the God of Fear, I rebuke thee! To the darkness in the depths, I rebuke thee!
-The Threefold Chant of Warding
description: Once per scene, you may raise a symbol of your god and channel divine warding magic. All Nightmares, Undead, and Void creatures must make a Morale check or flee. On a success, these creatures are instead Impaired for their next attack.

name: Divine Protection
flavorText:
Through faith, I will withstand.
-Ursine proverb
description: Gain +1 Armor (Max 4)

name: Soothe Pain
flavorText:
The Gods cannot give back what was taken from you, but they can give you the strength to keep moving.
-Warden Philippa
description: Once per session, when you offer comfort or guidance to one of your allies, restore d6 to the chosen attribute of that ally.

name: Divine Form
description: Gain a random Divine Mutation from the Mutations table.

name: Steadfast Beacon
flavorText:
I am the Light in the Deep, I am the Calm in the Storm.
-Final words of Delver Ptholos, Martyr
description: Spend 2 Stress to cast this spell. Until the end of the current scene, Allies who can see and hear you ignore the effects of Fear, and Nearby Monstrosities, Nightmares, and Void Creatures take d4 damage each time they act.

name: Revitalize
description:
Spend 2 Stress to cast this spell. Replenish half of maximum Guard to nearby Allies.
If you spend a Blessing to cast this spell, restore all Guard to nearby Allies.

name: Blessings of the Ancients
description:
Spend 2 Stress to cast this spell. While you concentrate on this spell, nearby Allies gain a Boon on all Action Rolls.
If you spent a Blessing to cast this spell, while you concentrate, nearby Allies ignore the next Curse they would receive from an Action Roll

name: Transcendence
description: To use this ability, die. When you die, your soul and essence transcend to the Divine Dream. Choose one of your Domains. You become a minor deity of that Domain. You can grant divine blessings to other Faithful that worship or pay homage to you. Once per campaign, you can perform a miraculous action of divine intervention on behalf of your surviving party members.

name: Called to Serve
description: You retire your character. They become an NPC that devotes their life to serving the Church of the Ancients in an elevated capacity. The rest of your party can call upon you once for a grand favor in a time of need. You live out the rest of your days in contentment and peace.

name: Blindside
flavorText:
The unseen blade strikes deepest.
-Bastian Proverb
description: Gain the Trickery domain. When you attack a creature that is distracted or unaware of your presence with a d4 or d6 weapon, add +d8 to the roll

name: Subterfuge
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time.

name: Trade Skills
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time.

name: Misdirect
flavorText:
With the right distraction, casting aside suspicion is as easy as discarding a cloak.
-Speaker Clade Manual of Fieldcraft
description: Gain Deception as a Skill. Spend 2 Stress to shift the attention of a person or creature to another PC or significant NPC.

name: Secret Technique
flavorText:
Met a fellow from Zerzuza who could break bones with just his fingers. So I broke his first.
-Grant Half-Snout, Guild Master at Arms
description: When you attack, spend 2 Stress to add a Gambit to your attack without spending a die from your attack pool.

name: Concealed Contingency
description: Increase the number of Curios you can safely carry by +1

name: Envenom
description:
Gain the Poisons skill. Once per session, you may apply poison to a weapon to add the Toxic tag and one of the following effects to the next attack made with the weapon:
Paralysis: A target of folk size or smaller cannot take any actions and loses all Guard. They may attempt a save against the effect at the end of their turn.
Neurotoxin: Add 2d12 Toxic damage to the attack
Blinding Powder: The target is Blinded, their attacks are Impaired and ranged attacks against them ignore Guard.

name: Ambush
flavorText:
Those who fight fair, lose.
-Quorric ibn Quorr, Veteran Delver
description: When you attack a creature who is unaware of you, ignore their Armor.

name: Dirty Tricks
description: When performing a Gambit, you may re-roll another die from your Attack Pool. If that die is a 4 or higher, you may use it for another Gambit. You may not use it for the Damage die.

name: Disguise
description: Through mundane means, you can spend 2 stress to disguise your appearance to appear as another member of your ancestry, including specific people. The disguise passes casual inspection, only intense scrutiny or pitched battle will reveal it as a disguise.

name: Warm Shadow
description: While in dim light, shadow, or darkness, you can spend 2 stress to wrap yourself in shadow and become invisible for up to 5 minutes. Inflicting harm, forcing a save, taking damage, or casting a spell will cancel the invisibility.

name: The Ultimate Heist
description: In a legendary act of theft, you steal one thing of your choice. Any single thing. It could be an object, an idea, a concept, an entire nation, whatever it is, you have absconded with it and it is yours to do with as you please. At the end of the next session or two, the former owner arrives to reclaim it, with fatal consequences for your character.

name: Spare Tools
flavorText: The right tool in the right place at the right time
description: Gain the Technology domain. You always count as having 1 bag of Supplies in your inventory. Once you use up these Supplies, you must spend at least one day resting in a Haven before you can use this ability again.

name: Theoretical Design
description: Gain one of the following domains. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new domain each time.

name: Applied Science
description: Gain one of the following as a Skill. You can gain this Talent more than once, choosing a new skill each time.

name: Jury Rig
flavorText:
If time is of the essence, choose function over perfection.
-Lectures of Professor Einwitz, Guild Artisan
description: Once per session, you can fix any broken object, but it only works once before it is destroyed beyond repair.

name: Containment Protocol
description: Increase the number of Curios you can safely carry by +1.

name: Field Modifications
flavorText:
Caught Feldyr tinkering with my crossbow. Was gonna rip into him, then I saw him shoot a bolt through a damn boulder. Gave him my axe next.
-From the journal of Roga, Veteran Delver
description:
Once per session, when you have an hour or two to spare in a safe or defensible location, you can spend that time modifying and enhancing your allies’ gear to better face upcoming challenges. Choose one of the options below. You and all other characters who let you modify their gear gain this benefit until you reach another safe place to rest.
One weapon gains +d6
One piece of gear grants a Boon when used in a relevant Action roll
One piece of armor or clothing grants +1 Armor
One piece of armor or clothing grants +4 Armor against a specific type of special damage (fire, lightning, poison, etc)

name: Magitech Augment
flavorText:
The body is a machine. Machines were meant to be improved.
- 2nd Treatise of Guild Artisan Oro Klein, Guild special collections.
description: You’ve replaced a part of your body with experimental Magitech. Choose one.

name: Deus Ex Machina
flavorText:
They will know my genius. They will remember my name.
- Final Treatise of Guild Artisan Oro Klein, Guild special collections.
description: Combining spare parts, Curios, and arcane forces, you cobble together a prototype device of immense power. When used, it permanently alters the reality of a location in a way of your choosing before self-destructing and erasing both you and the device from existence.

name: Chronostream Diverter
flavorText:
If you’re reading this, I haven’t written it yet.
-Anomalous message recovered from the Site 17 Incident
description: You activate the prototype artifact you’ve been obsessively working on for months and disappear from the timestream. Remove your character from the game. At some point in the future, in a scene where the party is facing a dire threat, you reappear as an immensely powerful, otherworldly version of your future self and remove a major obstacle for the party before disappearing once more into the timestream.

name: Phobophage
flavorText:
We do not just smell your fear. We breathe in its intoxicating aroma. We taste the sweetness of terror, the richness of despair. We devour it as our God will devour  you.
-Last words of Arnak Bonesplinter, Lapine raider, moments before execution
description: Gain the Nightmare domain. Once per session, you can learn someone’s greatest fear. If you use this as leverage against them, restore d6 Mind or Spirit.

name: Decayed Form
description: Gain a random Nightmare Mutation from the Mutations table. You can take this talent more than once.

name: Mark of the Devourer
flavorText:
Legends say that Lapine warriors possess an unnatural stamina, as if they draw strength from slaughter. Based on my observations of the vital signs of Prisoner 441 during these tests, I can conclude this is no myth.
-Sage Bran Keltor, Guild Archivist
description: Your shadow grows longer and sometimes moves of its own accord. You can sense when others nearby are afraid, and you find the sensation invigorating. When you end the life of a creature while it is feeling fear, clear 1 Stress.

name: Bone Minion
flavorText: <PLACEHOLDER>
description: <PLACEHOLDER>

name: Void Essence
flavorText:
…The creatures exhibited remarkable fluid defensive abilities, further studies will be necessary to develop a countermeasure.
-Report from The Bantar Incursion
description: Gain the Void domain. Once per session, if you would be wounded, you may instead take no damage and disperse your body into jet black ichor that slithers to a nearby location and reforms.

name: Mutable Form
description: Gain a random Void Mutation from the Mutations table.You can take this talent more than once.

name: Mark of the Void
flavorText:
A new window opens, the first of three, the third of five.
-Teachings of the Five-Eyed Wyrm
description: A third eye opens on your forehead, only you and others marked by the void can see it. It shifts in color depending on your mood. When you open it, you can see colors and phenomena that normal kin cannot perceive. You can see other folk’s auras and tell if someone else is marked by the Void, Nightmare, or a specific God. Additionally, you can see the auras of invisible creatures and objects.

name: Reverse Gravity
flavorText: <PLACEHOLDER>
description: <PLACEHOLDER>

name: Herald of the Five-Eyed Wyrm
flavorText: <PLACEHOLDER>
description: <PLACEHOLDER>
